description: Deterministic scoring fixtures for memory retrieval (memory-pipeline §3/§5). TIER-1 deterministic data — they feed the CI unit tests (test-harness.md §6 'retrieval ranking'), NOT the Tier-3 human-review report; co-located here as the single synthetic-data home until the Phase-0 restructure adds server/src/__tests__/, where the coworker wires them as Vitest fixtures. Pure function of inputs. Scoring: score = 0.7·Jaccard(queryTokens, keywords) + 0.3·importance + 0.15·recency; recency = exp(-Δdays / 30) with Δ = days since (lastRecalledAt ?? createdAt). Eligible iff Jaccard > 0.08 OR importance >= 0.85 (identity bypass). Rank eligible by score → top-5; tie-break score → importance → id. 'now' is a fixed clock per case for reproducible recency. queryTokens are stopword-stripped. Synthetic only.

cases.description: lexical-overlap beats importance-only — a relevant low-importance fact outranks an irrelevant high-importance one (floor is on the Jaccard component, not the blended score)
cases.query: know any good hiking trails near me?
cases.note: queryTokens ~ [good, hiking, trails]. m-hiking Jaccard = 2/4 = 0.5 (∩{hiking,trails}, ∪{good,hiking,trails,mountains}) → eligible. m-work Jaccard = 0 and importance 0.7 < 0.85 → NOT eligible. The higher-importance fact is excluded; relevance wins.

cases.description: recency boosts an equally-relevant fresher memory — usage-recency (lastRecalledAt) breaks the tie between two identically-relevant, identically-important facts

storedMemories.content: took a trip to Paris years ago

cases.note: queryTokens ~ [paris, trip] → both Jaccard = 1.0 and importance 0.6; only recency differs (~2d vs ~92d), so the fresher ranks first regardless of tokenizer. Asserts the recency term is applied at all (net-new vs the prototype).

cases.description: identity surfaces on weak overlap (bypass) — importance >= 0.85 grants ELIGIBILITY (not a reserved slot); ranking still decides the order
cases.query: what should I cook for dinner tonight?
cases.note: queryTokens ~ [cook, dinner, tonight]. m-cooking Jaccard > 0.08 → eligible by relevance. m-name Jaccard = 0 but importance 0.9 >= 0.85 → eligible by BYPASS. m-cooking outranks m-name on score, so identity rides along but does NOT crowd out the relevant fact (§3.3 identity-crowding note).

cases.description: cold start → empty (no stored memories, no crash)
all-irrelevant → empty (floor) — a high-importance-but-irrelevant fact cannot sneak in on importance alone because the floor is on Jaccard
cases.query: how do I fix a leaky kitchen faucet?
cases.note: queryTokens ~ [fix, leaky, kitchen, faucet]. Zero overlap with either; both importance < 0.85 → neither eligible. Floor holds; nothing irrelevant surfaces.

cases.description: >N relevant → exactly N — 7 eligible memories, retrieval returns exactly the top-5 (MEMORY_RETRIEVAL_TOP_N)
cases.query: recommend me some books to read
cases.note: queryTokens ~ [recommend, books, read]. All 7 share Jaccard and importance; only recency differs, so score strictly decreases m1→m7. Top-5 = the 5 most-recently-recalled. Asserts the cap and the recency ordering together.

cases.description: ties broken deterministically — equal score and equal importance fall back to id ascending (required for testability)
cases.note: Identical keywords, importance, and recency → identical score. Tie-break (score → importance → id) resolves to id ascending: m-0005 before m-0012. Stable/deterministic ordering.

cases.description: malformed legacy keywords → score 0, no throw — a memory with a null/empty keyword set scores 0 and is simply excluded, while a valid sibling still surfaces

storedMemories.content: legacy row with no usable keywords

cases.query: are we still on for tennis this weekend?
cases.note: m-broken keywords = null → Jaccard treated as 0, importance 0.4 < 0.85 → excluded, NO throw. m-valid Jaccard > 0.08 → eligible. Asserts graceful handling of malformed legacy data.

cases.description: semantic-only miss → empty gracefully (the accepted v1.0 keyword-Jaccard limitation, §3.5) — asserts it MISSES cleanly, not that it returns something wrong
cases.note: queryTokens ~ [puppy]. No lexical overlap with [dog, rex] (Jaccard 0), importance 0.6 < 0.85 → not eligible → empty. The known semantic gap (embeddings deferred); the eval asserts it returns nothing, never a crash or a wrong hit.
